revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine |Image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{Location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === Annual Festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related Locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == Location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:kk]]{{Omonoimi Faith}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

[[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == Location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

[[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]]

Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:11]]

[[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

[[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January[[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote worship site. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}[[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 |名称 = 物忌奈命神社 |画像 = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[東京都]][[神津島村]]41{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 物忌奈命{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[府社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月1日]]・[[8月2日|2日]]{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = 二十五日神事（[[旧暦1月24日]]-[[旧暦1月25日|25日]]）
[[神津島のかつお釣り行事|かつお釣り]]（[[8月2日]]） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=物忌奈命
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|一の鳥居}}]] '''物忌奈命神社'''（ものいみなのみことじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[神津島村]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。 [[神津島]]の鎮守で「神津島の史跡めぐりと神々にまつわる話」物忌奈命神社項。、前浜集落北部の丘上に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱。 * '''物忌奈命''' （ものいみなのみこと） *: 『[[続日本後紀]]』によると、三嶋神（[[伊豆国]][[一宮]]の[[三嶋大社]]祭神）三嶋大社祭神には[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]（おおやまつみのみこと）説と[[事代主命]]（ことしろぬしのみこと）説がある（現在の祭神の公称は両神）。神津島では事代主命として解説する例が多いが、本項では地名に基づく通称「三嶋神」として解説する。とその本后の阿波咩命（[[阿波命神社]]祭神）の間の[[御子神]]という『日本の神々』物忌奈命神社・阿波命神社項。。 [[鎌倉時代]]末期の成立とされる『[[三宅記]]』では、三嶋神が神集島（神津島）に置いた「長浜の御前」から長子「'''たゝない王子'''（たたない王子）」、次子「たふたい王子」が生まれたと記す。これら3神の社はそれぞれ阿波命神社、物忌奈命神社、[[#関係地|日向神社]]に比定される一方、島の伝承では物忌奈命神社と「たゝない王子」を別とし、「長浜の御前」の御子神は3柱であるとする。この中で「たゝない王子」は[[祇苗島]]（ただなえじま：神津島の東方に所在）の社に比定される（以上、「神津島の史跡めぐりと神々にまつわる話」阿波命神社より）。。 平成12年の震災で圧壊した本殿から「集島定大明神」(づしまさだめだいみょうじん？)の[[扁額]]が見つかる。 [[ファイル:Tokyo kouzushima shrine gate board.jpg|代替文=しょういちいづしまさだめだいみょうじん　じんぎどうかんれいうらべのあそんよしなが|サムネイル|正一位集島定大明神　神祇道管領卜部朝臣良長]] == 歴史 == [[File:Kozushima island.JPG|thumb|280px|right|{{center|[[神津島]]全景}}{{small|中央は[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）に大噴火を起こした[[天上山 (東京都)|天上山]]。その下の浜辺（長浜）に[[阿波命神社]]が、右端の集落に物忌奈命神社が鎮座する。}}]] === 概史 === 創建は不詳。 国史の初見は『[[続日本後紀]]』の[[承和 (日本)|承和]]7年（[[840年]]）における記事『続日本後紀』承和7年（840年）9月23日条。で、上津島（[[神津島]]）に坐す神は阿波神は三嶋大社本后である旨、物忌奈乃命はその御子神である旨、そしてこの神々のため神宮四院が新たに造営された旨が記載されている。同記事では、続いて神院の様子が描写される。そして、去る承和5年（[[838年]]）7月5日夜に神津島で激しい噴火が発生したといい、占いの結果、それは三嶋大社の後后が位階を賜ったにもかかわらず、本后たる阿波神には沙汰がないことに対する怒りによるものだと見なされた。同記事にある「後后」とは、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]の[[伊古奈比咩命神社]]祭神を指すとされており　、先の天長9年（[[832年]]）には三嶋神・伊古奈比咩命両神を名神に預けるという記事『釈日本紀』15所収『日本後紀』天長9年（832年）5月22日条。が載っている。 上記の承和7年の記事を受けて、約一ヶ月後に阿波神・物忌奈乃命両神の[[神階]]は無位から従五位下に昇った。その後はいずれも阿波咩命とともに、[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）に従五位上が授けられたのち、同年には[[官社]]に列し、[[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）には正五位下に昇った。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では、[[伊豆国]][[賀茂郡]]に「物忌奈命神社 名神大」と記載され、[[名神大社]]に列している『東京都の地名』物忌奈命神社項。。現在の東京都の中で、名神大社は物忌奈命神社と阿波命神社のみである。 [[中世]]の『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』（[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）以前成立）では物忌奈命神社を具体的に示す記載は見えないが、一説に「正一位天満天神」がこれにあたるとされる{{Sfn|増訂豆州志稿 巻八上|p=8}}。この中で「天満天神」とは、国府における物忌奈命神社の遥拝所であった三島市の[[三嶋大社#元摂末社|天神社]]（三嶋大社元摂社）を指すとされる{{Sfn|増訂豆州志稿 巻八上|p=8}}。 [[近世]]には、物忌奈命神社は「定大明神」とも呼称された。『式内社調査報告』物忌奈命神社項。 [[明治]]11年（[[1878年]]）9月1日、[[近代社格制度]]において[[府社]]に列した。 [[平成]]12年（[[2000年]]）7月1日の新島・神津島近海地震による地滑りで[[本殿]]が、その数日後の台風による地滑りで[[社務所]]兼参集殿が倒壊した[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ 物忌奈命神社]（神津島の神社）。が倒壊した本殿から「正一位集島定大明神」の扁額が発見された。 その後、平成18年に社務所兼参集殿、本殿とも再建された。 === 神階 === * 六国史における[[神階]]奉叙の記録 ** [[承和 (日本)|承和]]7年（[[840年]]）10月14日、無位から従五位下 （『[[続日本後紀]]』）『続日本後紀』承和7年（840年）10月14日条。 - 表記は「物忌奈乃命」。 ** [[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）10月8日、従五位上 （『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）『日本文徳天皇実録』嘉祥3年（850年）10月8日条。 - 表記は「物忌奈乃神」。 ** 嘉祥3年（850年）11月1日、官社に列す （『日本文徳天皇実録』）『日本文徳天皇実録』嘉祥3年（850年）11月1日条。 - 表記は「物忌奈神」。 ** [[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）12月15日、正五位下 （『日本文徳天皇実録』）『日本文徳天皇実録』仁寿2年（852年）12月15日条。 - 表記は「物忌寸奈命神」。 ** [[斉衡]]元年（[[854年]]）6月26日、正五位下 （『日本文徳天皇実録』）『日本文徳天皇実録』斉衡元年（854年）6月26日条。 - 表記は「物忌奈命神」。仁寿2年の記事と内容は重複。両記事の扱いには諸説ある。 * 六国史以後 ** 正一位か （『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』）{{Sfn|増訂豆州志稿 巻八上|p=8}} - 表記は「天満天神」。
伊豆主要神の神階　年表
