Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}) and held the rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島|12=draft}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) : According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社|12=draft}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft|12=draft}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft|12=draft}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft|12=draft}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft|12=draft}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft|12=draft}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島|12=draft|12=draft}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社|12=draft|12=draft}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft|12=draft}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft|12=draft}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft|12=draft}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島|12=draft}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社|12=draft}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|12=draft}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|12=draft}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島|qq=draft}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社|qq=draft}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinai-sha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzushima Village|qq=}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzushima Village|qq=}}, 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzushima Village|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzushima|qq=}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzushima Village|qq=}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kōzushima Village history]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzushima Village|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|Kōzushima|ja|神津島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzushima|qq=}}, situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三嶋大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōzushima Village|ja|神津島村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzushima Village|qq=}}, 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]]

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:神津島村の建築物]] [[Category:神津島村の歴史]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[en:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:神津島村の建築物]] [[Category:神津島村の歴史]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}
