{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Myojin-taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:神津島村の建築物]] [[Category:神津島村の歴史]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}

[[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:神津島村の建築物]] [[Category:神津島村の歴史]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:神津島村の建築物]] [[Category:神津島村の歴史]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}

revision.comment: idk how I ended up mistagging these but they are metropolitan shrines (Fu-sha) not Prefectural Shrines (Ken-sha). I do not believe Ken-sha existed in the cities of Kyoto Tokyo or Osaka

The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:神津島村の建築物]] [[Category:神津島村の歴史]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|lt=Fu-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōzushima Village]] [[Category:神津島村の歴史]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōzushima Village]] [[Category:History of Kōzushima Village]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11570647]] Wikidata name: Monoimina no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.2085|N|139.1346111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|lt=Fu-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |prefecture= {{ill|Kozushima|qid=Q1346527}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in the Islands of Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōzushima Village]] [[Category:History of Kōzushima Village]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}

The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in the Islands of Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōzushima Village]] [[Category:History of Kōzushima Village]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11570647]] Wikidata name: Monoimina no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.2085|N|139.1346111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |prefecture= {{ill|Kozushima|qid=Q1346527}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in the Islands of Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōzushima Village]] [[Category:History of Kōzushima Village]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11570647]] Wikidata name: Monoimina no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.2085|N|139.1346111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |prefecture= {{ill|Kozushima|qid=Q1346527}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in the Islands of Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōzushima Village]] [[Category:History of Kōzushima Village]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in the Islands of Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōzushima Village]] [[Category:History of Kōzushima Village]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11570647]] Wikidata name: Monoimina no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.2085|N|139.1346111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |prefecture= {{ill|Kozushima|qid=Q1346527}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in the Islands of Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōzushima Village]] [[Category:History of Kōzushima Village]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11570647]] Wikidata name: Monoimina no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Monoiminanomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja haiden.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.2085|N|139.1346111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Monoiminanomikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Sacred Body = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 1 & 2{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Association}} |Rituals = Nijūgohin Festival (24th-25th Day of the Lunar Calendar)
{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} (August 2) |Map = {{location map|Japan|label=Monoiminanomikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |prefecture= {{ill|Kozushima|qid=Q1346527}} }} [[File:Monoiminanomikoto-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] Monoiminanomikoto Shrine (物忌奈命神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and held the Rank of Prefectural Shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary shrine of [[:en:Kōzu-shima|Kōzushima]], situated on a hill in the northern part of the Maehama settlement."Exploring the Historic Sites and Myths of Kōzushima," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. == Deities == The enshrined deity is as follows: * Monoiminanomikoto (ものいみなのみこと) **According to the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'', Monoiminanomikoto is described as the child of [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Shrine]]’s deity (Mishima-no-Ōkami) and his consort Awahime.The deity of Mishima Shrine is interpreted as either Ōyama-tsumi or Kotoshironushi, with both officially enshrined today. In Kōzushima, the deity is often identified as Kotoshironushi. == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. The first historical reference is found in the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihongi]]'' entry for the year 840 (Shōwa 7), which states that the deity Monoiminanomikoto resides on Kōzushima and that shrines (Jingū Shiin) were built for these deities. In 838 (Shōwa 5), Kōzushima experienced a significant volcanic eruption on Tenjōzan, attributed through divination to the wrath of Mishima’s consort Awahime being excluded from court Rank acknowledgment."Japanese Gods," Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. Following this event, both Awahime and Monoiminanomikoto were elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in 840. The shrine later appeared in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 as a {{ill|Myojin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}. In modern times, the shrine was recognized as a Prefectural Shrine on September 1, 1878, under the Modern typeing System.''Shikinaisha Research Reports,'' Monoiminanomikoto Shrine Section. === Notable Events === In 2000, earthquakes and typhoons caused landslides that destroyed the shrine’s main hall and administrative buildings. The main hall was rebuilt in 2006.[http://www15.ocn.ne.jp/~ko-jinja/ Monoiminanomikoto Shrine] (Kōzushima Shrines). == Rituals == === festival === The annual festival is held on August 1 and 2, featuring the ''{{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}}'', a simulated ritual depicting traditional bonito fishing. === Nijūgohin Festival === Held from the 24th to 25th of the Lunar Calendar, this ritual involves nighttime prayers and processions, marking a sea-based deity welcoming ceremony. == Cultural Heritage == === National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|ja|神津島のかつお釣り行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima|qq=|1=Ritual Bonito Fishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590475}} - Designated on December 21, 1999.{{National Cultural Heritage Database|302|713|Katsuobushi Ritual of Kōzushima}} === Kōzushima Village Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Hall and Worship Hall (architecture) - Designated on January 10, 1970. == Related locations == [[File:Hyuga-jinja (Kozushima) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hyuga Shrine}}]] * Hyuga Shrine - Believed to enshrine the sibling of Monoiminanomikoto, located in Takō Bay, Kōzushima Village. [[File:Ten-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Ten Shrine (Mishima City)}}]] * Ten Shrine - Former branch shrine of Mishima Taisha, possibly related as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. == location == * [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Kōzushima, Tokyo|Kōzushima Village]], 41 == Notes == {{reflist}} Annotations {{reflist|group="注"|2}} Sources {{reflist|group="原"|2}} References {{reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}} * [http://kouzujinja.web.fc2.com/ Kōzushima Shrines] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|物忌奈命神社|version=104165071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Monoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[ja:物忌奈命神社]] [[sn:onoiminanomikoto Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in the Islands of Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōzushima Village]] [[Category:History of Kōzushima Village]] {{Commons category|Monoiminanomikoto-jinja}}
